name: reviewpilot-vscode
description: Local AI code review for the current project — runs the ReviewPilot engine on your working changes / branch diff / whole project and lists findings you can click to jump to and fix. Works in VS Code and Cursor.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No findings yet.
[Review Project](command:reviewpilot.review)
Choose a scope (working changes, branch diff, or whole project) when prompted.

reviewpilot.engine.description: Review engine to run locally. claude-code/cursor/codex invoke the matching CLI; claude-agent uses the Claude Agent SDK (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); mock is a credential-free no-op for testing the UI.

reviewpilot.command.description: Override the executable for the selected CLI engine (e.g. an absolute path to `claude`). Empty = use the engine's default command.

reviewpilot.args.description: Override the arguments passed to the CLI engine before the prompt. Empty = use the engine's default args.

reviewpilot.model.description: Model name passed to the engine when supported (e.g. for claude-agent). Empty = engine default.

reviewpilot.timeoutMs.description: Hard timeout for a single review invocation, in milliseconds. 0 = no timeout.

reviewpilot.defaultMode.description: Default scope pre-selected when you run Review Project. working = uncommitted changes vs HEAD; branch = current branch vs a base branch; full = the whole project.

reviewpilot.baseBranch.description: Default base branch used by the 'branch' review scope.

reviewpilot.onlyChangedLines.description: For working/branch scopes, keep only findings that fall on lines the diff actually changed (noise reduction).

reviewpilot.reviewFocus.description: Optional emphasis for the review (e.g. 'focus on SQL injection and N+1 queries').

reviewpilot.structuralContext.description: For working/branch scopes, enrich the prompt with code-review-graph structural analysis (risk-scored hotspots, test gaps, affected flows). Best-effort: skipped silently if the graph tool is unavailable.

reviewpilot.crgLauncher.description: Launcher that exposes the `code-review-graph` package on PATH (e.g. 'uvx', or an absolute path to a wrapper). Used only when structuralContext is enabled.
